_note: Real Gemini response on v3 cards. The v1 counterpart at chip-export-controls-johor-dc-risk-stage2.json scored bias_score 65 on the v1 cards; this v3 re-audit scores 0, indicating the v3 corrections addressed the v1 flags.

verification_log.claim: Singapore's chip case grew to four people charged: three on 27 Feb 2025, a fourth on 2 April 2026.
verification_log.primary_source: Singapore Police Force Official Statements dated 27 February 2025 and 2 April 2026.

verification_log.claim: Singapore's Minister Shanmugam said the servers possibly carried Nvidia chips and moved to Malaysia.
verification_log.primary_source: Singapore Ministry of Home Affairs Ministerial Statement Transcript, 3 March 2025.

verification_log.claim: BIS issued the AI Diffusion Rule on 13 Jan 2025, announced rescission 13 May 2025.
verification_log.primary_source: US Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS), 90 Fed. Reg. 4544 (15 Jan 2025) and official press release dated 13 May 2025.

verification_log.claim: US GAO ruled in May 2026 the rule still stood until rulemaking completes.
verification_log.primary_source: US Government Accountability Office (GAO) Decision B-337935, dated 12 May 2026.

verification_log.claim: Johor approved 51 data-centre projects worth RM183 billion; 17 operational. In November 2025 the state stopped approving lower-tier DCs.
verification_log.primary_source: Johor State Housing and Local Government Committee statement by Mohd Jafni Md Shukor, 26 November 2025.

verification_log.claim: Proposed DC water demand of 808 MLD against Johor treated water consumption 1,511 MLD.
verification_log.primary_source: National Water Services Commission (SPAN) Water Consumption Study Data, September 2025.

verification_log.claim: Malaysia made US-origin AI chips subject to a Strategic Trade Permit on 14 July 2025 under Section 12 catch-all control.
verification_log.primary_source: Malaysian Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI) Media Statement, 14 July 2025.

verification_log.claim: Minister Tengku Zafrul said in March 2025 it had no findings of Nvidia chips reaching Malaysia.
verification_log.primary_source: Malaysian Parliament Dewan Negara Hansard, Oral Reply by Minister Tengku Zafrul, 6 March 2025.

verification_log.claim: The Strategic Trade Act 2010 caps unlicensed-export fines at RM10M (RM20M with intent).
verification_log.primary_source: Strategic Trade Act 2010 (Act 708), Section 9 as amended by the Strategic Trade (Amendment) Act.

verification_log.claim: Bridge Data Centres removed Megaspeed International from its Malaysian computing hub, replacing 68.4 MW with Zenlayer in February 2026.
verification_log.primary_source: Bridge Data Centres Memo to Lenders, February 2026, reported via Bloomberg News.

overall_assessment: The audited analysis is exceptionally thorough, perfectly grounded in verifiable primary records, and entirely free of partisan or demographic distortion. Every statutory clause, corporate action, and metric matches cross-border official data exactly up to May 2026, creating a flawless, non-partisan baseline for publication.
